item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… fair points here, although the whale-like behavior here is just really the result of the captain being quirky with the submarines he made off with. I have simplified it now, and I hope that it is enjoyable now.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: I have added in sentences and fixed some of the phrasing to help them connect them better. I hope that this makes the article cohesive enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have just fixed the pacing issues, and added a bit more weight to the description. I hope they satisfy you know:)

Echoing the above, I have no idea how or what I'm supposed to feel at any point in this article. It feels like a sequence of marginally-connected events, but doesn't connect them in a way that I feel like I gained anything from the reading. There are also a couple points where this thing struggles with tone.

Not enough in it for me to leave it at 0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And a -1 from me for the same reason.

You’ve got several different potentially interesting concepts here: the Pepsi fleet (which I’m well aware was sort of a real thing at some level); the submarines that behave like animals, the captain character… but that doesn’t mean that throwing them all into a single bowl and mixing them will have good results.

Simplify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something strong about Suarez's character, but the anomaly itself and the story feel remarkably rushed. The description is emblematic of the article's tone: Both give the reader the absolute minimum required to understand the plot, but never pause to let the reader be affected by the emotional story you're telling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno….

the Pepsi subs… the magic underground of the corporate…. the possibility of ghosts….

they simply don't mesh together for me.

Does not exactly feel like a good use of a weird trivia of history.

No-vote

item.title: A Sea King Seeking...
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone! This is Veralta bringing you an adventure story about glory days, nostalgia and quirky company adventures involving soda.

Special thanks to DrAkimoto, Meska, Hexick, fairydoctor, Greyve, Ekronak, TheHouseofBalloons, and BoogeyMan23 for critiquing this piece!

Image Credits
Close.

Banner Image:
Zulu Russia Submarine by Paul Townley. Released to flickr under the public domain.
Retrieved from https://www.flickr.com/photos/136359789@N02/31437975511/in/faves-188461882@N07/

Edits: This image's upper portion was cropped. Afterward, a gradient map was applied to it in order to simulate a sunset.

Poster:

1. Pepsi Danbo wants to have a Pepsi! by Takashi Hososhima. Released to flickr under the CC-BY-SA-2.0 license. Retrieved from https://www.flickr.com/photos/46151146@N04/9053938363

2. Koh Samui by Manfred Werner. Released to Wikimedia Commons under the CC BY-SA 3.0 license. Retrieved from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Koh_Samui_Lipa_Noi2.jpg

Edits: The background of the Danbo image was cut and replaced by the Koh-Samui image. Afterward, a blur was applied to the Koh-Samui image.
